CheckTitle: AI agent service principals must carry all required governance tags
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Permissions**. Verifies that all IAM roles and service accounts used by the AI agent carry the full set of required governance tags (e.g. `owner`, `environment`, `data-classification`, `cost-centre`, `agent-id`). Tags are the foundation of Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC), cost attribution, and automated compliance reporting.
Risk: Untagged agent service principals cannot participate in ABAC policies, are invisible to cost-attribution and chargeback systems, and may escape automated compliance scans. An attacker who provisions an untagged role can bypass tag-based SCP controls and avoid detection by resource-inventory monitoring.

Code.Other:
1. List current tags on the agent role: `aws iam list-role-tags --role-name <role>`.
2. Identify missing required tags by comparing against the governance tag policy.
3. Add missing tags: `aws iam tag-role --role-name <role> --tags Key=owner,Value=<team> Key=environment,Value=<env>`.
4. Enable AWS Tag Policies in your organization to enforce required tags on new roles.
5. Use AWS Config rule `required-tags` to detect roles missing governance tags.
6. Update the agent manifest `all_resources_tagged` field to `true` after remediation.

Recommendation.Text: Enforce governance tags on all AI agent IAM roles using AWS Organizations Tag Policies and AWS Config rules. Include tagging requirements in the IAM role provisioning IaC templates. Use tag-based SCPs to deny actions by untagged principals, creating a self-enforcing tag compliance mechanism.

Notes: ASPM-021 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
